newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. Structural overhang confirmed: SKYT is pinned to the IonQ acquisition (announced Jan 26, 2026; $35.00/share cash-and-stock, ~$1.8B; shareholders approved May 8; FTC second request issued Apr 24 extends HSR review; close still guided Q2/Q3 2026). At $35.23 vs the $35.00 deal value, today's -4.4% is merger-arb/flow-driven, tracking the IONQ stock-consideration leg, not fundamentals. Annual meeting June 10 is procedural and will be cancelled if the deal closes first.

sources.label: IonQ to Acquire SkyWater Technology ($1.8B, $35/share) - IonQ press release
SkyWater stockholders approve IonQ merger (May 8, 2026) - Business Wire
FTC Second Request on IonQ/SkyWater merger (Apr 24, 2026 8-K) - StockTitan
SkyWater 2026 proxy: June 10 annual meeting amid pending IonQ merger - StockTitan

verdicts.rankRationale: On an alpha list that rewards hidden, asymmetric bottleneck upside, SKYT no longer qualifies: it trades at $35.23 against a $35.00 takeover price, so standalone CPO/advanced-packaging upside is capped at deal consideration while an unresolved FTC second request leaves real deal-break downside. The asymmetry is inverted relative to the page's thesis.
verdicts.bull: The deal closes in Q2/Q3 2026 and the stock component gives some IONQ-linked upside; if the FTC blocks it, SkyWater keeps a strategically scarce US trusted-foundry franchise plus a likely break fee.
verdicts.neutral: Stock is a merger-arb stub pinned near $35; daily moves track IONQ shares and spread mechanics, not the CPO/packaging thesis the page is ranking.
verdicts.bear: FTC second request drags or kills the deal and the stock retraces a large part of its deal-driven YTD run, with the standalone story still loss-making (Q1 net loss widened to $12.3M).
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FTC clearance / HSR waiting-period expiry enabling a Q2-Q3 2026 close
verdicts.primaryRisk: Deal break on antitrust review, removing the $35 floor and forcing a re-rate to standalone fundamentals

verdicts.rankRationale: The sovereign-AI foundry asset itself stays strategically relevant, but the page tracks investable equity leverage and SKYT's ticker is set to disappear into IonQ by Q2/Q3 2026 with value capped at the $35 cash-and-stock consideration; a watchlist slot at rank 14 overstates remaining leverage to the theme.
verdicts.bull: Until close, the stock leg of the consideration provides quantum/sovereign-compute optionality, and a deal break would leave a scarce US-flagged specialty foundry (Fab 25 doubled revenue to $160.7M in Q1) back in play standalone.
verdicts.neutral: Sovereign supply-chain fit is unchanged, but the equity now expresses merger-arb spread and IONQ beta rather than US specialty-foundry scaling exposure.
verdicts.bear: Deal closes and the listing is delisted with capped returns, or the FTC review stretches and the arb spread widens with no thesis-relevant payoff either way.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Merger completion or a definitive FTC outcome in Q2-Q3 2026
verdicts.primaryRisk: Ticker absorption into IonQ eliminates direct exposure; deal break re-prices the equity well below the $35 pin
